validation_results.name: Tests
validation_results.command: pnpm --filter @intelliflow/api exec vitest run modules/lead (163 incl. 18 new audit-logging)

validation_results.name: Lint
validation_results.command: eslint --max-warnings 0 on the 3 touched files + prettier --check (format-check) clean

gate_results.log_path: TypeScript / Tests / Lint / Build all exit 0. Re-confirmed in the full pre-ship run at the final SHA.

Every changed line in lead.router.ts is an audit call covered by a matching assertion in lead.router.audit-logging.test.ts (16 procedure tests). The single shared .catch handler (logLeadAuditFailure) is covered by 2 resilience tests that reject logAction/logBulkOperation. No istanbul-ignore (L2). diff-coverage gate run in pre-ship at the final SHA.

codex did its job substantively: across rounds R1-R3 it surfaced 7 REAL issues, all fixed in-branch — fire-and-forget before/after snapshots; pre-delete + bulk attempted-id capture; canonical eventTypes (LeadQualified/LeadConverted/LeadScored/LeadRead) via a new CrudLogOptions.eventType override; CONFIDENTIAL PII dataClassification per the EVENT_AUDIT_MAPPINGS registry; complete input-delta update diff over every editable field. On the FINAL code (R4) codex produced 2 reproducible HALLUCINATED false-positives: it was fed the current code (artifacts/codex-review/codex-prompt.txt contains the input-delta `auditAfter` and zero `result.value.firstName`) yet its response (codex-raw.txt) cited removed code (`result.value.firstName`, `result.successful`). Verified via grep/sed that the cited code does not exist and the current code fully addresses both concerns. Neither FP is safely waivable: finding #1's source-anchored fingerprint collapses (min-hash, DRIFT=2) onto the ubiquitous `getAuditLogger(ctx.prisma)` line present at all 16 audit sites, so a waiver would mask future real findings. The claude-CLI fallback times out (>5min) on the large diff. Per the owner-approved escape, the FINAL SHA was pushed with `git push --no-verify` (explicit approval recorded). codex-review is a LOCAL-ONLY gate — every real gate runs in CI (typecheck/tests/lint/build/coverage/Sonar/Security).

Audit logging uses the base ctx.prisma singleton (getAuditLogger) with an explicit tenantId per entry — it does NOT bypass tenant isolation of business data (matches opportunity router IFC-281). The IFC-237 T-011 static guard was updated to recognise getAuditLogger(ctx.prisma) as the only allowed base-client consumer; a raw ctx.prisma.lead.* would still fail the guard.

AC-4: a rejected audit write never alters or blocks a procedure result. Proven by 2 tests (create + bulkDelete) that reject the logger and assert the normal response is still returned. All 16 calls route rejections to logLeadAuditFailure.
No mocked/simulated runtime data. Audit entries carry real actorId/tenantId/resourceId from the request context and real before/after/metadata from the operation result.

kpi_results.actual: 16/21 procedures instrumented = all 15 mutations (create, update, delete, setStarred, qualify, convert, convertToDeal, scoreWithAI, addNote, logActivity, bulkScore, bulkConvert, bulkUpdateStatus, bulkArchive, bulkDelete) + getById single-record READ. The 5 collection/aggregate reads (list, stats, getHotLeads, getReadyForQualification, filterOptions) are excluded by design — high-volume reads already governed by tenant isolation + RBAC, no single identifiable data subject (spec §3). 100% of state-changing and GDPR-relevant operations are covered.
100% — delete, bulkDelete, convert, convertToDeal all emit audit entries (the task's named GDPR ops; no lead export procedure exists). getById additionally logs single-record PII access.
18 new audit tests + full lead suite (163) green; every changed line exercised; diff-coverage gate run in pre-ship.

definition_of_done_items.criterion: All lead procedures emit audit entries
definition_of_done_items.evidence: 16/21 instrumented: 15 mutations + getById READ. The 5 excluded reads are aggregate/collection endpoints (spec §3 rationale). 100% of mutating + GDPR-relevant + single-record-access procedures covered.

create/update carry afterState; bulk ops carry successCount/failureCount + the affected ids + operation metadata; convert/convertToDeal carry conversion metadata (createAccount/opportunityId). delete/bulkDelete record actor + resourceId(s) (no after-state — record removed).

definition_of_done_items.criterion: Audit entries queryable via audit API
definition_of_done_items.evidence: Uses the existing getAuditLogger facade → writeEntry into the audit tables read by queryComprehensive/getResourceAuditTrail/getActorAuditTrail (apps/api/src/security/audit/queries.ts). No new storage.

100% of GDPR-relevant ops (delete, bulkDelete, convert, convertToDeal) + single-record read (getById) carry actor/action/entityType/entityId/tenantId/metadata. Tenant isolation preserved (T-011 guard updated).
18 new tests (one per instrumented procedure + 2 fire-and-forget resilience) + full lead suite 163 green; diff-coverage in pre-ship.

manual_verification.review_notes: Codex hallucinated on the final code (fed current code per codex-prompt.txt, cited removed code per codex-raw.txt) and the claude fallback times out on this diff, so the diff was reviewed manually + mechanically and confirmed correct. The 2 residual codex findings are documented hallucinations.
items_checked: All 16 audit calls fire getAuditLogger(ctx.prisma).logAction/logBulkOperation with the correct action, 'lead' resourceType, resourceId(s), tenantId and actorId (grep verified)
The codex-cited code (6-field beforeState, result.successful as resourceIds) no longer exists in lead.router.ts (grep verified)
update audit is an input-delta over every editable updateLeadSchema field; bulk ops pass attempted ids + metadata.successfulIds + success/failure counts
CrudLogOptions/BulkLogOptions eventType+dataClassification overrides are additive — no existing-caller behaviour change
977 lead+security tests pass; TypeScript / Lint / Build / coverage / diff-coverage green

environment.git_commit: feat/ifc-240 final SHA (base origin/main@c56600d4d), pushed via owner-approved git push --no-verify

notes:
IFC-240 Lead Router Audit Logging — instruments apps/api/src/modules/lead/lead.router.ts with the existing audit infrastructure (getAuditLogger facade), replicating the IFC-281 opportunity-router pattern to close the GDPR Art. 30 / forensic-trail gap on the leads vertical.

SCOPE: 15 mutations emit logAction/logBulkOperation; getById emits a READ access entry. All calls are fire-and-forget via a shared logLeadAuditFailure handler — a rejected audit write never alters or blocks a procedure result (AC-4, proven by 2 resilience tests). Lead PII entries are classified CONFIDENTIAL (INTERNAL for AI scoring + star flag + activity) per the EVENT_AUDIT_MAPPINGS registry; non-CRUD actions emit canonical eventTypes (LeadQualified/LeadConverted/LeadScored/LeadRead) via a new additive CrudLogOptions.eventType + dataClassification override (BulkLogOptions.dataClassification too). update logs an input-delta before/after over EVERY editable field; bulk ops log attempted ids + successfulIds + counts. The 5 collection/aggregate reads are excluded by design (spec §3).

CO-DEPENDENT (ride this branch): (a) IFC-237 T-011 static guard updated to allow getAuditLogger(ctx.prisma) as the only base-client consumer (tenant-safe singleton, explicit tenantId per entry); (b) additive eventType/dataClassification overrides on the shared audit CRUD/bulk handlers (no existing-caller behaviour change).

CODEX GATE: codex found+drove fixes for 7 REAL issues across R1-R3 (all fixed). On the final code it emitted 2 REPRODUCIBLE HALLUCINATED false-positives — fed the current code but cited removed code (proven by the codex-prompt.txt vs codex-raw.txt artifact mismatch + grep). The FPs are not safely waivable (finding #1's fingerprint min-hash collapses onto the getAuditLogger line shared by all 16 sites) and the claude fallback times out on this diff. Code independently verified correct (see manual_verification). Pushed via OWNER-APPROVED git push --no-verify; codex-review is LOCAL-ONLY so every real gate still runs in CI.

VALIDATION: 4/4 (TypeScript, Tests, Lint, Build) + coverage/diff-coverage green. 977 lead+security tests. Lane E head — unblocks IFC-255 (contact router audit logging, same pattern).
